# PI Cloud Control Plane

PI supports two operating modes. Both use the same dashboard UI and the same pi-agent binary — only the environment variables differ.

---

## Mode 1: Local / Same-Wi-Fi (default)

No relay server needed. Dashboard and pi-agents must be on the same network.

```
pi-agent  ──→  dashboard (port 3000)
browser   ──→  dashboard (port 3000)
terminal  ──→  dashboard (port 14801)
```

**Setup:**
```bash
# packages/web/.env.local  (no relay vars needed)
PI_ACCESS_TOKEN=your-dashboard-password
```

**Pairing command (generated by dashboard):**
```
pi-agent pair --server http://192.168.x.x:3000 --code XXXX --start
```

---

## Mode 2: Cloud Control Plane

PI Relay is a persistent cloud server that both the dashboard client and pi-agents connect to outbound. Neither side needs a fixed IP or open inbound port.

```
pi-agent  ──→  PI Relay (public)  ←──  dashboard client
browser   ──→  dashboard (local)   ──→  PI Relay API
terminal  ──→  PI Relay /pi-agent-relay  ←──  dashboard
```

### Deploy the relay (Fly.io)

```bash
cd packages/relay

# 1. Create a persistent volume BEFORE first deploy
fly volumes create pi_relay_data --size 1 --region sin

# 2. Deploy
fly launch --name pi-relay --region sin
# or on subsequent deploys:
fly deploy

# 3. Set secrets
fly secrets set \
  PI_RELAY_TOKENS="daemon-abc123:daemon,pi-xyz789:pi" \
  PI_RELAY_OWNER_TOKEN="your-dashboard-bearer-token"
```

> **Warning:** Without the volume, the SQLite database lives on Fly's ephemeral filesystem and is wiped on every deploy. Always create `pi_relay_data` before the first deploy.

**Optional env overrides** (set via `fly secrets set` or `[env]` in fly.toml):
```
PI_RELAY_DB_PATH=/data/pi-relay.db   # default
PI_RELAY_PORT=8787                   # default
PI_RELAY_HOST=0.0.0.0               # default
```

### Configure the dashboard (cloud mode)

```bash
# packages/web/.env.local
PI_ACCESS_TOKEN=your-dashboard-password
PI_RELAY_BASE_URL=https://pi-relay.fly.dev
PI_RELAY_PI_TOKEN=pi-xyz789
PI_RELAY_DAEMON_TOKEN=daemon-abc123
PI_RELAY_PUBLIC_WS_URL=wss://pi-relay.fly.dev
```

When `PI_RELAY_BASE_URL` + `PI_RELAY_PI_TOKEN` are both set, the dashboard automatically uses the relay as its backend. Local mode is the fallback when either is missing.

### Pairing a new machine (cloud mode)

The dashboard generates relay-aware pair commands automatically. In cloud mode the pair command uses the relay URL:

```
pi-agent pair --server https://pi-relay.fly.dev --code XXXX --start
```

pi-agent stores the relay URL and token in its state file after pairing. All subsequent heartbeats and reports go to the relay directly — the dashboard is not in the loop for those.

---

## Token format

`PI_RELAY_TOKENS` is a comma-separated list of `token:kind:label` entries:
```
daemon-abc:daemon:my-machines,pi-xyz:pi:dashboard
```

- `daemon` tokens: used by pi-agent (heartbeat, register, report, enrollment consume)
- `pi` tokens: used by the dashboard (overview, jobs, approvals, enrollments)

---

## Architecture summary

| Route | Caller | Mode |
|-------|--------|------|
| `POST /v1/daemon/register` | pi-agent | cloud relay |
| `POST /v1/daemon/heartbeat` | pi-agent | cloud relay |
| `GET /v1/daemon/agents/:id/poll` | pi-agent | cloud relay |
| `POST /v1/daemon/jobs/report` | pi-agent | cloud relay |
| `POST /v1/daemon/requests` | pi-agent | cloud relay |
| `POST /v1/daemon/enrollments/consume` | pi-agent pair | cloud relay |
| `GET /v1/pi/overview` | dashboard | cloud relay |
| `POST /v1/pi/jobs` | dashboard | cloud relay |
| `POST /v1/pi/approvals/:id/respond` | dashboard | cloud relay |
| `GET /api/remote-agents/*` | pi-agent (local) | local dashboard |
| `ws://../pi-agent-relay` | pi-agent terminal | relay WebSocket |

**`PI_RELAY_PI_BASE_URL` is deprecated.** The relay no longer calls back into the local dashboard. Remove it from any existing configs.

---

## SQLite notes

The relay uses SQLite (WAL mode) as its persistent store. At 100 agents heartbeating every 5s, write throughput is ~20 writes/sec — well within SQLite's limits.

To migrate to Postgres later, only the DB layer (`packages/relay/src/db.ts` + `store.ts`) needs to change. The API surface stays the same.
